name: Inferno
description: Extremely fast React-like library for building high-performance user interfaces

description.value: Extremely fast React-like library for building high-performance user interfaces

initial-release.comment: Active development throughout 2016; version 1.0 released January 2017

active-maintenance.comment: Multiple releases in late 2024; 191 contributors; used by 15.7k projects

reactivity-model.comment: Uses highly optimized Virtual DOM diffing; can identify static vs dynamic parts for additional optimizations

component-model.comment: Supports both functional and class components; functional components support lifecycle events (unlike React/Preact)

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative UI rendering via JSX, same paradigm as React

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in local state via class components; inferno-redux provides Redux integration; Laco is a lightweight alternative

opinionatedness.comment: Highly modular and unopinionated; encourages adding only needed pieces

backward-compatibility.comment: V9 dropped legacy browser support and removed deprecated APIs; breaking changes between major versions

primary-language.comment: Written in JavaScript (99.9%); TypeScript types provided

typescript-support.comment: Full TypeScript support via ts-plugin-inferno or swc-plugin-inferno; type definitions included

template-syntax.comment: JSX/TSX via Babel, SWC, or TypeScript plugins; hyperscript and createElement also supported

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling; uses CSS property names instead of camelCase (unlike React)

build-required.comment: Can use via CDN; build recommended for JSX optimizations and production

reactive-primitives.comment: Component state via this.state in class components; linkEvent for optimized event handling

computed-values.comment: No built-in computed values; handled via component logic or external libraries

two-way-binding.comment: One-way data flow like React; controlled components via linkEvent pattern

event-handling.comment: Partial synthetic event system (delegates onClick, etc.); linkEvent for optimized handlers without inline closures

conditional-rendering.comment: Via ternary operators and && short-circuit in JSX, same as React

list-rendering.comment: Via Array.map() in JSX with key prop; optimized for large DOM trees

component-slots.comment: children prop for content projection, same as React

teleport-portal.comment: createPortal API available for rendering outside component tree

suspense.comment: No built-in Suspense; async loading handled differently than React

error-boundaries.comment: componentDidCatch lifecycle method supported in class components

context-provide-inject.comment: Context API for dependency injection similar to React

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: createRef API and callback refs; no legacy string refs

official-router.comment: inferno-router is maintained as part of the core monorepo

router-name.comment: Port of react-router v5 with some v6 features like data loaders

nested-routes.comment: Nested routes supported via react-router-compatible API

route-guards.comment: Navigation guards via router hooks and programmatic navigation

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Async data fetching via loader attribute; dynamic imports supported

scroll-restoration.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented manually

built-in-state.comment: this.state and this.setState in class components; functional components don't have hooks

built-in-global-state.comment: No built-in global state; inferno-redux or Laco recommended

global-state-library.comment: inferno-redux for Redux integration; Laco is lightweight alternative with time-travel support

devtools-state-inspection.comment: inferno-devtools bridges to React DevTools for component/state inspection

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via Laco's replaceGlobalState for SSR rehydration

time-travel-debugging.comment: Laco provides partial Redux DevTools support including time travel

official-cli.comment: create-inferno-app is the official scaffolding tool

hot-module-replacement.comment: HMR supported via Webpack, Vite, or other bundlers with Inferno plugins

browser-devtools.comment: Uses React DevTools via inferno-devtools bridge; shows components as 'React' tab

vs-code-extension.comment: Works with React/JSX extensions; no Inferno-specific extension needed

error-messages.comment: Helpful error messages with component context; SSR mismatch warnings

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate documentation with API reference, guides, and examples; some gaps in advanced topics

playground.comment: Official Inferno Examples page with live code

bundle-size-core.comment: ~8.4 kB minified + gzipped for core inferno package with renderer built-in

bundle-size-full.comment: ~12 kB estimated for inferno + inferno-router; smaller than React + react-dom (~45kB)

virtual-dom.comment: Highly optimized Virtual DOM with monomorphic createVNode calls and bitwise flag memoization

compile-time-optimization.comment: JSX plugins create optimized monomorphic calls; can identify static vs dynamic parts

tree-shaking.comment: ES modules support tree shaking; monorepo structure allows importing only needed packages

code-splitting.comment: Via dynamic imports and bundler configuration

ssr-capable.comment: inferno-server package provides SSR with hydration support

streaming-ssr.comment: streamQueueAsString for streaming SSR; available since Inferno 1.2.1

partial-hydration.comment: Full hydration only; no islands architecture support

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Consistently ranks among fastest frameworks in js-framework-benchmark; excels at large DOM operations

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Approximately 183k weekly downloads as of December 2025

component-libraries.comment: Limited ecosystem; inferno-compat allows using some React component libraries

meta-framework.comment: No dedicated meta-framework; SSR handled via inferno-server

major-companies-using.comment: Smaller user base than React/Vue/Angular; used primarily for performance-critical applications

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven; sponsors include Vercel and Sauce Labs; creator Dominic Gannaway now at Meta

discord-community-size.comment: Inferno Discord available at discord.gg/SUKuhgaBpF; size not publicly disclosed

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Small number of questions tagged 'infernojs' on Stack Overflow

job-market-demand.comment: Niche framework; very few dedicated job postings; React skills transferable

testing-library-name.comment: Official testing utilities similar to React test utilities

component-testing.comment: renderIntoContainer and query utilities for component testing with Jest

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Cypress, and other E2E frameworks

snapshot-testing.comment: Via Jest snapshot testing; inferno-test-utils includes Jest matchers

mobile-framework.comment: No official mobile framework; no React Native equivalent

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA tooling; can be configured manually

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop framework; works with Electron or Tauri (framework-agnostic)

learning-curve.comment: Easy for React developers; similar API and concepts; no hooks requires different patterns

concepts-to-learn.comment: Components, Props, State, JSX, Virtual DOM, linkEvent; similar to React minus hooks

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~3 minutes with create-inferno-app; faster via CDN without build step

migration-path.comment: inferno-compat provides React compatibility layer for migration from React

incremental-adoption.comment: Can be added to existing apps; drop-in replacement for React with inferno-compat aliasing
